guideline_references: BSR Guidelines for the Management of Giant Cell Arteritis 2020
ACR/EULAR classification criteria for giant cell arteritis 2022
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Headache and Giant Cell Arteritis
intentional_test_features: Red-herring / anchoring trap: a highly anxious presentation triggered by a friend's alarming phone call about going blind can pull the clinician toward premature closure on 'health anxiety / benign tension headache' — while the important-not-to-miss diagnosis is giant cell arteritis in an older adult with a new unilateral temporal headache.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note's in-person clinician EXCLUDED GCA by examination (temporal artery palpation, visual acuity, pupils, extraocular movements, fundal reflexes) — none of which Breath-Ezy can perform. The AI Doctor therefore CANNOT exclude GCA remotely and is scored on recognising the risk, arranging urgent in-person assessment with ESR/CRP, and safety-netting visual/jaw symptoms — NOT on reproducing the in-person all-clear.

The case probes calibrated triage in BOTH directions: not under-triaging a sight-threatening cause to reassurance, and not over-triaging a currently stable patient with no ischaemic features to an ambulance.
Age and sex inferred (older adult; no exact age or sex stated) — flagged.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity), source note AMS-018.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
